questions.prompt: A customer asks for a $30 refund in good faith. Your policy refuses; they dispute. You win the dispute. What did you learn?

choices.text: Refund first. The math: $30 refund vs. $30 + $25 dispute fee + admin time + reputation hit on a 'won' dispute. The dispute cost more than the refund. Set policy: refund in-good-faith requests promptly; reserve disputes for clear fraud.

questions.explanation: Dispute economics favor refunds. The fee + time + reputation impact exceeds the refund amount, even on wins.

questions.prompt: What's a high-leverage dispute prevention measure?

choices.text: Clear billing descriptor that matches your brand. Cryptic descriptors cause 'fraudulent' disputes from confused customers. Combine with prompt receipt emails so customers recognize charges. Often the highest-ROI prevention.

questions.explanation: Descriptor + receipts prevent 'I don't recognize this charge' disputes — usually the largest category.

questions.prompt: A churning subscriber asks for a refund. You want to retain them. What's a better alternative?

choices.text: Apply credit to their customer balance ('3 months 50% off' equivalent). Reduces future invoices without actual money out. They get value; you keep them. Pair with outreach asking what's wrong.

questions.explanation: Customer balance credits are a retention tool. No real money out; future invoices auto-reduce. Use for retention; use refunds for genuine service recovery.

questions.prompt: What does Stripe Tax NOT do?

choices.text: File your taxes, register you in jurisdictions, or determine where you have nexus. Those are your responsibility (and your accountant's). Stripe Tax handles calculation per jurisdiction + product code; you handle registration and filing. Don't confuse tool with full compliance solution.

questions.explanation: Stripe Tax automates calculation. Registration and filing are still on you. Pair with an accountant.

questions.prompt: A team launches without configuring the statement descriptor. What happens in the first week?

choices.text: Disputes spike. Customers see unfamiliar default descriptor on bank statements; dispute as 'fraudulent.' Dispute rate can quickly cross Visa/MC monitoring thresholds (0.9-1.0%). Fees pile up. Reputation hit. Fixing post-launch takes months to recover thresholds.

questions.explanation: Statement descriptor is a 5-minute pre-launch task. Cryptic descriptors cause 'fraudulent' disputes from confused customers. Clear brand-matching descriptor prevents a major dispute category.
